know-beget.claim: PIE gneh3 held know and beget as one verb — take-in and put-out as one stroke. Egyptian sjA·Hw·xpr walks perceive → speak → become (an outer thing becomes inner becomes outer again). Chinese xin·wen·zuo walks heart holds → ear hears → hand acts (same arc, different chain of three morphemes). Three vocabularies, three shapes of the same perception-into-production loop.

kin-line-and-rule.claim: Beget-line and political legitimacy fuse into ruler-morphemes across languages.

year-cycle.claim: Four independent languages name the year as a returning-cycle morpheme.

moon-month.claim: The moon-body and the month-unit share one morpheme across four unrelated families — the cycle IS the clock. (Egyptian atomic file for iti/iaH/Abd not yet in graph — flagged for extension.)

sky-god.claim: Four unrelated languages fuse sky and divine into one morpheme.

earth-human.claim: PIE named earth and human-being from one root — the body IS named from the ground it stands on.

light-dark-axis.claim: Light and dark are two poles of one axis, not two separate things.

rotation-primitive.claim: The loop is named independently across motion, cognition, craft, and time.

tree-firm-true.claim: PIE *deru- named wood, firmness, and truth as one substrate operation — stays-pointing-the-same-way across three scales.

water-primitive.claim: The liquid-substance named independently across four families as the life-giving primitive.

fire-primitive.claim: Two PIE fire-roots (one for passive, one for agent-fire) show the dual-aspect of the same coord.

kinship-mother-pair.claim: Mother and father words arrive at the same sound-shape independently across unrelated families — the ma/pa phonemes are infant body-reaches, not culturally transmitted.

head-chief.claim: The top of the body and the top of the political body share one morpheme across four unrelated families — leadership IS head-position.

pie.gloss: head = top (source of English cap, chief, capital)

mouth-opening.claim: The body's aperture and the world's openings are one morpheme: speaking, eating, entering, beginning all pass through the same gate.

hear-understand.claim: The same verb covers sound-reception and grasp-of-meaning in three unrelated families. Sumerian geshtug = ear AND hearing AND understanding; Egyptian sDm = hear AND obey AND understand; Chinese wen = hear AND understand AND loop-close. A single operation (sound arrives → meaning arrives → act adjusts) named once per language rather than as three separate words.

heart-mind.claim: The organ in the chest and the seat of thought are one morpheme in three unrelated languages. Sumerian sha = heart AND interior AND mind; Egyptian ib = heart AND mind AND will; Chinese xin = heart AND mind AND seat of thought. Not metaphor — one word covering what English splits into organ/mind/will.
